title: Garlicky French Wagyu Rillettes
description: A rich, creamy, and indulgent French dish made with Wagyu beef, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This recipe is suitable for a Buddhist temple setting, as it does not include any ingredients that are typically avoided.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Season the Wagyu beef with salt and black pepper.
Grill the Wagyu beef for 2-3 minutes per side, or until it reaches a medium-rare temperature.
Transfer the Wagyu beef to a cutting board and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, melt the garlic butter in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the onion to the saucepan and cook for 5 minutes, or until it is translucent.
Add the garlic to the saucepan and cook for 1 minute, or until fragrant.
Add the wine to the saucepan and cook for 1-2 minutes, or until the liquid has almost completely evaporated.
Add the stock to the saucepan and bring to a simmer.
Add the thyme and bay leaves to the saucepan and simmer for 5-7 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened.
Shred the Wagyu beef with two forks and add it to the saucepan.
Simmer the rillettes for 5-7 minutes, or until they are heated through and the flavors have melded together.
Stir in the parsley and season the rillettes with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the rillettes to a serving dish and serve immediately.
Serve the rillettes with toasted bread or crackers, if desired.
